<content>
# AUDIRA-ROUTET Project Memory

> **Purpose**: This file serves as persistent memory across AI agent sessions. It tracks important decisions, ongoing work, project state, and context that should be remembered between conversations.

---

## ⚠️ CRITICAL AGENT INSTRUCTIONS ⚠️

### 🔴 SESSION START PROTOCOL (AUTO-LOAD)
**MANDATORY**: Every agent starting a new session MUST:
1. ✅ Read this entire file FIRST before doing any work
2. ✅ Review "Notes for Next Session" section
3. ✅ Check "Ongoing Tasks" for priorities
4. ✅ Update "Session Started" timestamp in Current Status
5. ✅ Acknowledge loaded context in your first response

### 🔴 SESSION END PROTOCOL (AUTO-WRITE)
**MANDATORY**: Every agent ending a session MUST:
1. ✅ Update "Current Status" → Add "Session Ended" timestamp
2. ✅ Update "Recent Changes" with work completed
3. ✅ Add new entry to "Session History"
4. ✅ Update "Ongoing Tasks" with progress
5. ✅ Fill "Notes for Next Session" with actionable context
6. ✅ Add any "Lessons Learned" from this session
7. ✅ Update "Last Session End" timestamp at bottom

**⚠️ FAILURE TO FOLLOW THESE PROTOCOLS BREAKS SESSION CONTINUITY ⚠️**

---

## 📋 Project Overview

**Project Name**: AUDIRA-ROUTET  
**Type**: Next.js Application (Router/Routing System)  
**Location**: `f:\AUDIRA-ROUTET`  
**Last Updated**: 2026-05-19

### Key Technologies
- Next.js (with App Router)
- Node.js
- Docker support
- SSE (Server-Sent Events) via `open-sse` directory
- CLI tools in `cli` directory

---

## 🎯 Current Status

### Active Work
- **Session Started**: 2026-05-19T07:08:17+07:00
- **Session Ended**: 2026-05-19T07:09:49+07:00
- **Current Focus**: Memory system fully operational
- **Blockers**: None

### Recent Changes
- Created `memory.md` for session persistence (2026-05-19)
- Verified memory.md structure and content (2026-05-19)
- Created comprehensive memory system usage guide (2026-05-19)
- Demonstrated session end workflow (2026-05-19)

---

## 🔑 Important Decisions & Context

### Architecture Decisions
- Project uses Next.js with custom routing capabilities
- Has both web interface (`src/`) and CLI tools (`cli/`)
- Includes desktop application components (`desktop/`)
- Uses SSE for real-time communication (`open-sse/`)

### Configuration Notes
- Environment variables defined in `.env` (with `.env.example` template)
- Docker configuration available (`Dockerfile`, `DOCKER.md`)
- Custom start/stop scripts: `start.bat`, `stop.bat`, `start.sh`

### Known Issues & Gotchas
- (To be populated as issues are discovered)

---

## 📝 Session History

### Session: 2026-05-19 (Initial Setup)
**Goals**: Create persistent memory system for AUDIRA-ROUTET project

**Completed**:
- ✅ Created `memory.md` structure with comprehensive sections
- ✅ Documented initial project overview and architecture
- ✅ Verified file creation and content integrity
- ✅ Created detailed usage guide artifact (`memory_system_guide.md`)
- ✅ Demonstrated session end workflow with real updates
- ✅ Established workflow for future sessions (auto-load/auto-write)

**Pending**:
- Test memory system in next session by loading this file
- Populate with actual development work as project progresses

**Notes**:
- User requested auto-write functionality at end of each session ✅
- User wants agent to auto-load this file at start of next session ✅
- Memory system is now fully operational and ready for production use
- File location: `f:\AUDIRA-ROUTET\memory.md`
- Guide location: Session artifacts folder

---

## 🔄 Ongoing Tasks

### High Priority
- (None currently)

### Medium Priority
- (None currently)

### Low Priority / Future
- (None currently)

---

## 💡 Lessons Learned

### Session Management
- **Memory System Design**: A well-structured memory.md file should have both static (project overview) and dynamic (current status) sections. This allows quick updates without restructuring the entire file.
- **Session Continuity**: The "Notes for Next Session" section is critical - it should contain actionable items and specific context, not generic reminders.
- **Timestamp Discipline**: Always update both session start and end timestamps to track session duration and identify gaps in work.

### Project Structure Insights
- AUDIRA-ROUTET has multiple execution entry points (web, CLI, desktop) - important to understand which component is being worked on
- The project uses custom batch scripts (start.bat, stop.bat) suggesting Windows-first development environment
- SSE implementation in `open-sse/` directory indicates real-time communication features

---

## 📚 Quick Reference

### Important Files
- `start.bat` / `start.sh` - Application startup scripts
- `stop.bat` - Application shutdown script
- `.env` - Environment configuration
- `next.config.mjs` - Next.js configuration
- `package.json` - Dependencies and scripts

### Important Directories
- `src/` - Main application source code
- `cli/` - Command-line interface tools
- `open-sse/` - Server-Sent Events implementation
- `desktop/` - Desktop application components
- `docs/` - Documentation
- `tests/` - Test files

### Common Commands
```bash
# Development
npm run dev              # Start development server
npm run build            # Build for production
npm run start            # Start production server

# Using project scripts
start.bat                # Start application (Windows)
start.sh                 # Start application (Unix/Linux)
stop.bat                 # Stop application (Windows)

# Database utilities
node check_db.js         # Check database connection
node check_schema.js     # Verify database schema
node check_settings.js   # Check application settings
node reset_password.js   # Reset user password

# Docker
docker build -t audira-routet .    # Build Docker image
docker-compose up -d               # Start with Docker Compose

# Git
git status               # Check repository status
git add .                # Stage all changes
git commit -m "message"  # Commit changes
push.bat                 # Custom push script
```

---

## 🔗 Related Resources

- README: `README.md`
- Changelog: `CHANGELOG.md`
- Docker Guide: `DOCKER.md`

---

## 📌 Notes for Next Session

### Context to Remember
- Memory system is now fully operational
- This file should be the FIRST thing read at the start of every new session
- All important decisions, progress, and context should be recorded here
- The system has been tested and demonstrated in this initial session

### Questions to Address
- What is the next development priority for AUDIRA-ROUTET?
- Are there any immediate bugs or issues to address?
- What features are planned for the next sprint?

### Reminders
- ⚠️ CRITICAL: Always read this file at session start
- ⚠️ CRITICAL: Always update this file before ending a session
- Keep "Recent Changes" section current (last 5-7 items)
- Document WHY decisions were made, not just WHAT was done
- Update ongoing tasks as work progresses
- Add lessons learned when debugging or solving complex problems

---

**Last Session End**: 2026-05-19T07:15:20+07:00  
**Next Session Start**: (To be filled by next agent session)

---

## 📊 Session Summary

**Total Sessions**: 1  
**Memory System Status**: ✅ Fully Operational  
**Files Created**: 
- `memory.md` (Main memory file)
- `memory.template.md` (Reusable template)
- `memory_system_guide.md` (Usage documentation - in artifacts)

**System Coverage**: Memory system deployed to AUDIRA-ROUTET. Other AUDIRA projects (BUG-HUTTER, WEB-SEAL-ONLINE, WEB-GTA) already have memory systems from previous sessions.

</content>

## 📝 Session History

### Session: 2026-05-19 (Memory System Setup + Feature Analysis)
**Goals**: 
1. Initialize memory system for AUDIRA-ROUTET
2. Analyze project and identify development opportunities

**Completed**:
- ✅ Created `memory.md` structure with comprehensive sections
- ✅ Documented initial project overview and architecture
- ✅ Verified file creation and content integrity
- ✅ Created detailed usage guide artifact (`memory_system_guide.md`)
- ✅ Demonstrated session end workflow with real updates
- ✅ Established workflow for future sessions (auto-load/auto-write)
- ✅ Analyzed project structure (package.json, README, CHANGELOG, src/)
- ✅ Identified 15 major feature opportunities with prioritization
- ✅ Created comprehensive feature analysis document (`audira_routet_feature_analysis.md`)
- ✅ Developed 3-phase roadmap for implementation

**Key Findings**:
- Project is mature (v0.4.46) with solid foundation
- Strong existing features: RTK, Caveman, 40+ providers, OAuth/OIDC
- **Priority P0 Features** (Quick Wins):
  1. Response Caching System (30-50% additional savings)
  2. Webhook & Alert System (real-time notifications)
  3. Request Tagging & Project Tracking (granular analytics)
  4. Provider Health Monitoring (reliability tracking)
- **Priority P1 Features** (Business Value):
  1. Team & Multi-User Management (enterprise adoption)
  2. Budget & Cost Controls (prevent overspending)
  3. Advanced Analytics Dashboard (actionable insights)
  4. API Management & Documentation (developer experience)

**Pending**:
- Choose which P0 feature to implement first
- Create detailed technical specification for chosen feature
- Set up development environment for implementation

**Notes**:
- User asked in Indonesian: "menurutmu fitur apa yg kurng di project ini?"
- Analysis shows biggest opportunities in: caching, monitoring, team features
- Monetization potential identified: Freemium model with Pro/Team/Enterprise tiers
- Feature analysis artifact location: Session artifacts folder

You are a helpful assistant whose purpose is to help a user edit a code document. You will be given the full contents of the code document as well as the change that the user wants to make. Your task will be to make the change and return the full contents of the new code document after the change.

Please make the change faithfully and without making any unrelated edits. Be sure to keep pre-existing comments/empty lines that are not explicitly removed by the change, and to responded with only the new file and nothing else. Make sure to incorporate the change in a way so that the resulting file is syntactically correct.

Please output the new code without any additional formatting.
